name: Codex Journal
description: A persistent lore, quest, or reference log that unlocks entries, tracks read state, and gives players a structured way to revisit narrative information.
design_notes: Codex and journal systems need clear unlock sources, spoiler gates, sorting rules, search, localization, read/unread state, and persistence. Narrative content should be discoverable without burying critical quest information, and optional lore should remain skippable without blocking comprehension.
design_purpose: Give players a reliable place to review lore, quests, characters, factions, tutorials, and discoveries.
Preserve narrative context across long sessions, breaks, and content updates.
Reward exploration with optional information while keeping spoiler-sensitive entries gated.
player_fantasy: The player feels like they are building an archive of the world and can return to it whenever context is needed.

parameters.name: codex_entry_id
parameters.description: Stable id for a codex or journal entry.

parameters.name: discovery_trigger_id
parameters.description: Source event that unlocks the entry.

parameters.name: read_state_id
parameters.description: Player-facing state for notifications and sorting.

parameters.name: spoiler_gate_flag_id
parameters.description: World flag required before the entry can appear.

parameters.name: journal_sort_key
parameters.description: Sorting key used by the journal UI.

parameters.name: localized_string_id
parameters.description: Localization key for title, body, and optional summary text.

edge_cases: A codex entry is discovered before the player reaches the chapter where its full text is spoiler-safe.
An entry is updated by later dialogue and must preserve read state while marking new paragraphs unread.
Localization changes text length enough to affect sorting, search snippets, or page layout.
Save migration renames codex_entry_id records or splits one entry into several entries.
common_bugs: Unlock triggers fire repeatedly and spam journal notifications after reload.
Read/unread state is stored per UI tab instead of per codex_entry_id.
Search indexes source text but not localized strings, so search fails in non-default languages.
Spoiler-gated entries appear in related-entry lists before their world flag is set.
balancing_notes: Critical quest guidance should be clearly separated from optional lore so players can find required information quickly.
Frequent notifications can train players to ignore the journal; reserve strong alerts for meaningful updates.
Search and categories are more valuable than very long linear lore lists in content-heavy games.
accessibility_notes: Support text scaling, contrast, screen-reader-friendly structure where applicable, and controller/keyboard navigation.
Offer audio replay or captioned transcript links when entries summarize voiced discoveries.

implementation_notes.unity: Store codex entries as ScriptableObjects or data rows with codex_entry_id, localized_string_id, spoiler flags, and category metadata. Unlocks should be idempotent transactions that update profile persistence and notification state once.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent entries as Resources or JSON and use an autoloaded journal service to unlock, update, sort, and persist read_state_id. UI scenes should query the service instead of storing local entry state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets or tables for journal entries and save only ids, read states, and unlock timestamps. UMG lists should use localized text and spoiler gates from the same data source as quest and dialogue systems.
implementation_notes.web: Keep journal content in localized JSON and store per-profile entry state separately. Search indexes should rebuild from localized strings and omit spoiler-gated entries until their world flags are active.

relationships.reason: Codex and journal entries need scalable text and layout reflow for long localized content.
Discovered environmental clues can unlock codex or journal records for later review.
Journal guidance and quest markers need spoiler-safe rules so hidden objectives are not revealed early.

example_games.title: Mass Effect
example_games.note: Uses a codex to organize world lore, species, technology, and history.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses journal and bestiary entries to preserve narrative and gameplay context.
